title: Intern - Data Scientist (Analytics) - Mobility
description:
About Grab and Our Workplace

Grab is Southeast Asia's leading superapp. From getting your favourite meals delivered to helping you manage your finances and getting around town hassle-free, we've got your back with everything. In Grab, purpose gives us joy and habits build excellence, while harnessing the power of Technology and AI to deliver the mission of driving Southeast Asia forward by economically empowering everyone, with heart, hunger, honour, and humility.

You will apply a data-first approach to product and business thinking, unveil data-driven insights, design controlled experiments and be objective while evaluating results, all of this while working with stakeholders to evaluate new and innovative product and business ideas. You will contribute to helping Developers, Designers and Product Managers across the company with recommendations on how to learn from unexpected results, study underlying causes for them and iterate on the next set of products to build for our users.

This role is based on-site in our Petaling Jaya office.

The Critical Tasks You Will Perform
• You will collaborate with stakeholders to identify business requirements and outcomes.
• You will provide catered analysis for specific products and operations, define critical business metrics and track them rigorously, and provide regular recommendations to achieve continuous improvements
• You will define and align with stakeholders on key metrics, design and propose experimentation strategies.
• You will process and distill stakeholders requirements into concise insights through reports, presentations, and dashboards.
• You will develop data pipelines, customized data science models to solve identified problems.
• You will launch A/B tests, analyze experiment results and provide recommendations.

What Essential Skills You Will Need
• Only for candidates who are available to start in August 2026.
• Your studies as an Undergraduate in Computer Science, Data Science, Data Analytics Statistics, Mathematics, or similar majors.
• Foundation in SQL, Python, R or other scripting/programming languages to problem-solve.
• Data visualization and storytelling skills. Experienced in creating dashboards using PowerBI or Tableau will be a plus
• Strong statistical knowledge with hands-on experience running and analyzing controlled experiments.
• Keen eye for spotting patterns and trends in data, able to hypothesize problem statements and propose appropriate solutions

Life at Grab

We care about your well-being at Grab, here are some of the benefits we offer our Interns:
• Attractive Monthly Allowance!
• EPF (Employees Provident Fund) or SOCSO (Social Security Organization) where applicable.
• Balancing personal commitments and life's demands are made easier with our FlexWork arrangements such as differentiated hours

What We Stand For At Grab

We are committed to building an inclusive and equitable workplace that provides equal opportunity for Grabbers to grow and perform at their best. We consider all candidates fairly and equally regardless of nationality, ethnicity, race, religion, age, gender, family commitments, physical and mental impairments or disabilities, and other attributes that make them unique.

location: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur
description:
Are you ready to get ahead in your career?

• We want to empower you to turn your ambitions into achievements.
• We thrive in inclusiveness, diversity and embrace close collaborations for you to create impact for yourself and others.
• Together, we aim to bring the best of technology to help people, businesses and the nation to be ahead in a changing world.
• To realise our vision to become Malaysia’s leading converged solutions company, we are looking for a new talent to innovate and grow with us in a culture that values commitment, performance and possibilities.

Why does this job exist and why is it critical?

Join Maxis as a Data Analytics & AI Intern, where you will gain hands-on experience supporting data-driven initiatives, AI applications, and automation projects across the organisation. This role offers exposure to real-world analytics use cases, machine learning concepts, and process optimisation, contributing directly to innovation and operational efficiency in a fast-paced environment.

Key Responsibilities

• Support data preparation, cleaning, and analysis tasks.

• Work with datasets to identify trends and support decision-making.

• Contribute to improving data-driven solutions using basic AI concepts.

• Participate in automation initiatives to streamline workflows and improve efficiency.
• Explore opportunities to implement simple automation solutions.

Value-Added Exposure

• Gain hands-on experience with real-world data analytics and AI projects.
• Work with modern tools, technologies, and datasets in an enterprise environment.
• Learn how data and automation drive business decisions and innovation.
• Receive mentorship and guidance from experienced professionals.

Preferred Skills / Qualifications

• Proactive, eager to learn, and able to work independently and collaboratively.
• Good communication skills in English (spoken and written).

Why Join Us?

• Gain practical exposure to data analytics, AI, and automation in a leading organisation.
• Work on impactful projects that drive business value.
• Develop technical and analytical skills in a supportive environment.
• Build a strong foundation for a career in data, AI, or technology.

Internship Duration: 6 months, starting July 2026

What’s next?

• Once you’ve applied online, our team will carefully review your application. Due to a high volume of applications, we appreciate your patience to allow for a fair and timely review process.
• Should you be shortlisted for the role, we will send you an invitation via email for a digital interview. You can also check on your application status by logging into your candidate account.

Maxis values diverse voices & people. We hire and reward our employees based on capability & performance — regardless of ethnicity, gender, age, education, religion, nationality or physical ability.

title: Junior Data Analyst Internship
description:
Our Opening and Your Responsibilities
We are seeking an analytical, detail-oriented student or recent graduate to join our Data Governance Team as a Junior Data Analyst Intern. This position offers direct exposure to corporate data management strategies, automated data quality frameworks, and stakeholder reporting within a global IT environment.

• Data Quality: Help build and maintain automated data checks using SQL and KNIME to keep our data accurate and reliable.
• Dashboards: Support the creation and updating of data quality dashboards for our internal teams.
• Requirements: Work with product teams and senior stakeholders to turn business needs into clear technical tasks (user stories).
• Reporting: Help prepare monthly reports and presentations on data trends and insights for leadership.
• Documentation: Monitor reporting consistency and write clear guides, manuals, and technical documentation.

What You Need to Succeed
• Currently pursuing or recently completed a Diploma or Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related quantitative discipline.
• Familiarity with data manipulation tools such as Excel, SQL, or KNIME is highly advantageous.
• A strong willingness to learn and apply industry best practices regarding data quality, data ownership, and data definitions.
• Exceptional organizational skills with a structured approach to task management and follow-ups.
• Strong written and verbal English communication skills, with an ability to document technical processes clearly.
• High level of precision and diligence when executing routine or repetitive analytical tasks.

Our Offer to You
• Practical application of data governance principles within a multinational corporate structure.
• Direct mentorship from senior data professionals and exposure to enterprise-level data tools (SQL, KNIME).
• Experience in translating complex business requirements into technical developments.

About Mettler Toledo
METTLER TOLEDO is a global leader in precision instruments and services. We are renowned for innovation and quality across laboratory, process analytics, industrial, product inspection, and retailing applications. Our sales and service network is one of the most extensive in the industry. Our products are sold in more than 140 countries, and we have a direct presence in approximately 40 countries. For more information, please visit www.mt.com.
Equal Opportunity Employment
We promote equal opportunity worldwide and value diversity in our teams in terms of business background, area of expertise, gender and ethnicity. For more information on our commitment to Sustainability, Diversity and Equal Opportunity please visit us here.

title: Intern, AI, Automation & Data Analytics
description:
People Group/Office Administration Transformation

Type: Internship (3–6 months, flexible)

Application Deadline: 15 May 2026
Why This Internship Is Different

Most internships give you coffee runs and filing.

This one gives you the keys to automate, analyze, and visualize how People Group and Office Administration really works.

You won’t just “observe” – you will:
• Identify repetitive, manual processes and automate them with AI/RPA
• Build interactive dashboards (Power BI, Tableau, or similar) that turn raw People data into strategic insights for Management

title: Data Analyst - Identity Access Management (Internship)
description:
About the job
Job purpose
Are you passionate about technology and looking to kickstart your career in a world-leading insurance company? AXA Group Operations is offering an exciting opportunity for young, dynamic individuals to join our Identity Global Products’ teams (IGP).

As an intern, you will support the management of master data related to identity products and asset inventories, including establishing and maintaining accurate sources of truth through systems like CMDB (Configuration Management Database) and IT Asset Management (ITAM).

Additionally, you will assist in creating and enhancing reports for the authentication platform, enabling better monitoring and analysis of authentication activities tailored to stakeholders' needs.

Main missions
Your responsibilities include:

• Review existing master data related to identity management systems and asset inventories.

• Assist in data cleansing, deduplication, and standardization efforts.

• Support the documentation of data management processes, standards, and sources of truth, including how to maintain accurate hardware and software inventory data via CMDB and ITAM systems.

• Help ensure data security and compliance with relevant policies.

• Collaborate with Global IGP, security, and asset management teams to validate data accuracy and consistency.

• Gather requirements for reporting needs from stakeholders involved in security, finance, local IAM owners, and application owners.

• Customize reports based on stakeholder needs to support different groups’ insights.

• Automate report generation where possible to enable timely insights.

• Assist in creating dashboards or visualizations for easier interpretation of authentication data.

• Document reporting procedures and provide support for report users.

Expected skills & experience
We are looking for someone with the following skills:

Hard Skills:

• Familiarity with data management principles, sources of truth concepts, and asset inventories (e.g., CMDB, ITAM).

• Familiarity with reporting tools (e.g., Excel, Power BI, Tableau).

• Basic knowledge of SQL or other data querying languages.

• Basic understanding of identity management systems and their data requirements.

• Understanding of authentication mechanisms, security principles, and asset management.

Soft Skills:

• Strong attention to detail and accuracy.

• Good communication skills for interacting with stakeholders.

• Ability to document processes clearly.

• Proactive attitude and willingness to learn.

Background:

• Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's program in Information Technology, Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related fields.

Learning Opportunities

• Hands-On Experience with Identity and Asset Data: Understand how various identity management systems, CMDB, and ITAM tools work together to provide accurate sources of truth for user identities and hardware/software assets.

• Data Management Skills: Learn best practices for data cleansing, standardization, and maintaining reliable sources of truth across systems.

• Reporting and Data Visualization: Develop skills in creating tailored reports and dashboards for diverse stakeholder groups using tools like Power BI or ELK.

• Understanding Security and Asset Lifecycle: Gain insights into security policies, asset lifecycle management, and how accurate data supports compliance and operational decision-making.

• Stakeholder Engagement: Learn how to gather requirements from teams such as security, finance, IAM owners, and application owners, and tailor reports accordingly.

• Problem Solving & Analytical Thinking: Enhance your ability to analyze complex data and provide actionable insights.

• Project Management: Gain experience in planning, executing, and documenting tasks within a professional environment.

What we offer
We bring together the expertise, cultural diversity and creativity of over 8,000 employees worldwide and we’re committed to equal opportunities in all aspects of employment (gender, LGBT+, disabled persons, or people of different origins) and to promoting Diversity & Inclusion by creating a work environment where all employees are treated with dignity and respect, and where individual differences are valued.

About the entity
AXA is becoming a sustainable tech-led company and at AXA Group Operations we are one of the major catalysts for this transformation.
We set the tone by triggering and empowering the evolution of our insurance business model through technology and innovation, driving its concrete implementation globally at speed, with a high quality of advisory and execution.
We are present across 17 countries with committed, highly qualified teams. We leverage technology, data, sourcing, security and investment allocation in a global way, but also achieve economies of scale and synergies when necessary.

At AXA Group Operations, we want to be recognized in three fields of action:
• State-of-the-art Data Technology to drive customer experience
• State-of-the-art Procurement & Sourcing to drive efficiency and better manage risks
• High-Performing Global Team for stronger partnerships with AXA entities
About AXA
As a world-leading insurance company, we act for human progress by protecting what matters. With 153,000 employees in 54 countries working for 105 million customers, we’ve created a truly dynamic and vibrant community. Inclusion and diversity link closely with our values, and together we’re nurturing a culture of respect, for each other, for our customers and the communities around us. Join AXA and you’ll feel like you belong, are included and can thrive. You’ll be able to shape the way you work and truly grow your potential as you seek out new opportunities, push boundaries and benefit people in critical moments of their lives. This is your chance to build the tomorrow you want. Know you can.

title: KL Kuala Lumpur Internship- Data Analyst (Sales)
location: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur
description:
Location: Kuala Lumpur (Hybrid)

About Us: AI Sales Doctor is an early-stage pre-Seed Data & AI startup that help Tech partners find Asian clients with buying intent data, founded by ex-Google & Microsoft experts. We're looking for builders to join us to build and shape the future of sales function in era of AI together.

Our core values are 1) Customer Centricity and 2) Employees grow before the Company grow (clearly defined career path & promotion timeline due to our promote from within culture). We pride ourselves on our diverse and inclusive work culture, where every team member is valued and respected.

Remuneration: Monthly stipend MYR 1200 (Base 1000, guaranteed incentive MYR 200 subjected to work behavior (punctuality) & no performance issues.

Rewards: Good performers may get occasional business incentive trip and will be considered for full-time jobs in KL.

Job Description: We're hiring Data Analyst (Sales) Interns as part of AI Sales Career Launchpad Programme to support and execute modern, AI-assisted go-to-market (GTM) outreach strategies for B2B clients across Asia.

This is a structured 4 to 12-month internship designed to give you real-world experience in AI-assisted B2B outreach and GTM campaign execution.

You will not be doing “theory-based training.” You will work on live client campaigns under supervision and be guided with clear performance metrics.

Key Responsibilities:

Data Collection & Data Quality Assurance

• Research and identify relevant companies and decision-makers using AI-assisted tools

• Execute personalised, value-driven outreach via email, LinkedIn, and digital channels for data collection (get & check)

• Leverage AI & data tools to ensure data quality assurance.

Data Analyst for Sales & Performance Tracking

• Lead and take action on go-to-market campaigns for real B2B clients via phone, email and LinkedIn to immerse into Sales process, as building block for impactful Data analytics.

• Learn how GTM strategies are executed in real environments through hands-on work.

• Track outreach performance, response rates, and meeting conversions.

CRM & Data Hygiene

• Maintain accurate records of data collection outreach activities and prospect interactions.

• Update CRM systems consistently to ensure data quality and visibility.

• Review results regularly and refine outreach approaches based on feedback.

Who We’re Looking For

This role is suitable for students or fresh graduates who are:

• Curious about Data, AI, tech sales, or startups

• Strong communicators

• Proactive and disciplined

• Interested in exploring Data, GTM, digital outreach, and revenue operations

You may be a good fit if you:

• Communicate confidently and professionally in English and Bahasa Melayu

• Are comfortable initiating conversations and following up

• Are resilient, disciplined, and motivated by measurable goals

• Want to build a career in Data, AI, tech sales, or future entrepreneurship

No prior Data, GTM, AI, or tech sales experience is required. Training is provided on the job.

What You Will Gain:

• Hands-on startup exposure

• Practical experience with Data and AI-assisted GTM tools

• Real B2B outreach experience for foundation on Data collection & Data analytics

• Structured mentorship from founders

• Exposure to modern sales systems and revenue operations

High-performing interns may be considered for full-time conversion opportunities upon completion of the internship (subject to performance and business needs).

Leadership & Mentorship

You will work closely with the founding team led by Elton Lee (ex-Google, Microsoft, Oracle), bringing real-world enterprise and startup sales experience across Asia, including AI-led GTM initiatives.

Coaching is practical, execution-focused, and based on live work — not theory.

Work Setting

4 day work from office, 1 day work from home every week.

Free basic breakfast.

Ready to Apply? - Applications for this batch closes on 20 March 2026

If you’re looking for a real role with clear earning potential and career growth in Data, AI and tech sales, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now.
